message.content:
Update the Claude Code statusLine script at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh`:

1. Remove the hostname (keep username only, or remove user@host entirely if that looks cleaner)
2. Add current objective (the current task/goal Claude is working on)
3. Add progress percentage toward that objective

Read the current script first, then make the changes. Use appropriate colors and keep the line readable.

content.text: Let me read the current script and settings files first.

content.content:
1	#!/bin/sh
2	# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k Pure style
3	# Shows: user@host  cwd  git-branch*  model  agent  skill  ctx-remaining%
4	
5	input=$(cat)
6	
7	cwd=$(echo "$input"          | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
8	model=$(echo "$input"        | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
9	remaining_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
10	agent_name=$(echo "$input"   | jq -r '.agent.name // empty')
11	skill_name=$(echo "$input"   | jq -r '.output_style.name // empty')
12	
13	# user@host in grey (ansi 242)
14	user=$(whoami)
15	host=$(hostname -s)
16	
17	# Shorten home directory to ~
18	short_cwd=$(echo "$cwd" | sed "s|^$HOME|~|")
19	
20	# Git branch (skip optional locks)
21	git_branch=""
22	if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1; then
23	  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
24	  dirty=$(git -C "$cwd" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)
25	  if [ -n "$dirty" ]; then
26	    git_branch="${branch}*"
27	  else
28	    git_branch="${branch}"
29	  fi
30	fi
31	
32	# Context remaining indicator — shown in amber when low (<= 20%), green otherwise
33	ctx_part=""
34	if [ -n "$remaining_pct" ]; then
35	  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining_pct")
36	  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
37	    ctx_color="\033[38;5;214m"   # amber/orange — low context warning
38	  else
39	    ctx_color="\033[38;5;71m"    # muted green — healthy context
40	  fi
41	  ctx_part=" ctx:${remaining_int}%"
42	fi
43	
44	# Colour palette
45	grey="\033[38;5;242m"
46	blue="\033[38;5;75m"
47	teal="\033[38;5;73m"    # agent name
48	purple="\033[38;5;141m" # skill/output-style name
49	reset="\033[0m"
50	
51	# --- Build the line ---
52	
53	# user@host  cwd
54	printf "${grey}%s@%s${reset}  ${blue}%s${reset}" "$user" "$host" "$short_cwd"
55	
56	# git branch
57	if [ -n "$git_branch" ]; then
58	  printf "  ${grey}%s${reset}" "$git_branch"
59	fi
60	
61	# model
62	if [ -n "$model" ]; then
63	  printf "  ${grey}%s${reset}" "$model"
64	fi
65	
66	# agent (only when present — i.e. Claude was started with --agent)
67	if [ -n "$agent_name" ]; then
68	  printf "  ${teal}agent:%s${reset}" "$agent_name"
69	fi
70	
71	# skill / output style (suppressed when value is "default" to reduce noise)
72	if [ -n "$skill_name" ] && [ "$skill_name" != "default" ]; then
73	  printf "  ${purple}skill:%s${reset}" "$skill_name"
74	fi
75	
76	# context remaining
77	if [ -n "$ctx_part" ]; then
78	  printf "  ${ctx_color}%s${reset}" "$ctx_part"
79	fi
80	
81	printf "\n"
82

content.text:
Good. Now I have a clear picture. Here is what I will change:

- Remove `host` and the `user@host` block, keeping just the username (or dropping it entirely since solo username adds little value - I will keep just the username for context but make it cleaner)
- Add `session_name` from the JSON input as the "current objective" field
- Add `context_window.used_percentage` displayed as a progress bar toward that objective (used% as a proxy for progress through the task)
- Keep colours consistent and the line readable

input.old_string:
#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k Pure style
# Shows: user@host  cwd  git-branch*  model  agent  skill  ctx-remaining%

input=$(cat)

cwd=$(echo "$input"          | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
model=$(echo "$input"        | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
remaining_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
agent_name=$(echo "$input"   | jq -r '.agent.name // empty')
skill_name=$(echo "$input"   | jq -r '.output_style.name // empty')

# user@host in grey (ansi 242)
user=$(whoami)
host=$(hostname -s)

# Shorten home directory to ~
short_cwd=$(echo "$cwd" | sed "s|^$HOME|~|")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
git_branch=""
if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
  dirty=$(git -C "$cwd" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$dirty" ]; then
    git_branch="${branch}*"
  else
    git_branch="${branch}"
  fi
fi

# Context remaining indicator — shown in amber when low (<= 20%), green otherwise
ctx_part=""
if [ -n "$remaining_pct" ]; then
  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining_pct")
  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
    ctx_color="\033[38;5;214m"   # amber/orange — low context warning
  else
    ctx_color="\033[38;5;71m"    # muted green — healthy context
  fi
  ctx_part=" ctx:${remaining_int}%"
fi

# Colour palette
grey="\033[38;5;242m"
blue="\033[38;5;75m"
teal="\033[38;5;73m"    # agent name
purple="\033[38;5;141m" # skill/output-style name
reset="\033[0m"

# --- Build the line ---

# user@host  cwd
printf "${grey}%s@%s${reset}  ${blue}%s${reset}" "$user" "$host" "$short_cwd"

# git branch
if [ -n "$git_branch" ]; then
  printf "  ${grey}%s${reset}" "$git_branch"
fi

# model
if [ -n "$model" ]; then
  printf "  ${grey}%s${reset}" "$model"
fi

# agent (only when present — i.e. Claude was started with --agent)
if [ -n "$agent_name" ]; then
  printf "  ${teal}agent:%s${reset}" "$agent_name"
fi

# skill / output style (suppressed when value is "default" to reduce noise)
if [ -n "$skill_name" ] && [ "$skill_name" != "default" ]; then
  printf "  ${purple}skill:%s${reset}" "$skill_name"
fi

# context remaining
if [ -n "$ctx_part" ]; then
  printf "  ${ctx_color}%s${reset}" "$ctx_part"
fi

printf "\n"
input.new_string:
#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k Pure style
# Shows: user  cwd  git-branch*  model  agent  skill  objective+progress  ctx-remaining%

input=$(cat)

cwd=$(echo "$input"           | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
model=$(echo "$input"         | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
remaining_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
used_pct=$(echo "$input"      | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')
agent_name=$(echo "$input"    | jq -r '.agent.name // empty')
skill_name=$(echo "$input"    | jq -r '.output_style.name // empty')
session_name=$(echo "$input"  | jq -r '.session_name // empty')

# username only (no hostname)
user=$(whoami)

# Shorten home directory to ~
short_cwd=$(echo "$cwd" | sed "s|^$HOME|~|")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
git_branch=""
if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
  dirty=$(git -C "$cwd" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$dirty" ]; then
    git_branch="${branch}*"
  else
    git_branch="${branch}"
  fi
fi

# Context remaining indicator — shown in amber when low (<= 20%), green otherwise
ctx_part=""
if [ -n "$remaining_pct" ]; then
  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining_pct")
  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
    ctx_color="\033[38;5;214m"   # amber/orange — low context warning
  else
    ctx_color="\033[38;5;71m"    # muted green — healthy context
  fi
  ctx_part=" ctx:${remaining_int}%"
fi

# Objective progress bar — uses context used% as a proxy for task progress
# 10-block bar: filled blocks scale with used_pct
progress_part=""
if [ -n "$session_name" ] && [ -n "$used_pct" ]; then
  used_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$used_pct")
  # Number of filled blocks out of 10
  filled=$(( used_int / 10 ))
  empty=$(( 10 - filled ))
  bar=""
  i=0
  while [ $i -lt $filled ]; do
    bar="${bar}█"
    i=$(( i + 1 ))
  done
  i=0
  while [ $i -lt $empty ]; do
    bar="${bar}░"
    i=$(( i + 1 ))
  done
  progress_part="${bar} ${used_int}%"
fi

# Colour palette
grey="\033[38;5;242m"
blue="\033[38;5;75m"
teal="\033[38;5;73m"      # agent name
purple="\033[38;5;141m"   # skill/output-style name
yellow="\033[38;5;221m"   # objective label
gold="\033[38;5;179m"     # progress bar
reset="\033[0m"

# --- Build the line ---

# user  cwd
printf "${grey}%s${reset}  ${blue}%s${reset}" "$user" "$short_cwd"

# git branch
if [ -n "$git_branch" ]; then
  printf "  ${grey}%s${reset}" "$git_branch"
fi

# model
if [ -n "$model" ]; then
  printf "  ${grey}%s${reset}" "$model"
fi

# agent (only when present — i.e. Claude was started with --agent)
if [ -n "$agent_name" ]; then
  printf "  ${teal}agent:%s${reset}" "$agent_name"
fi

# skill / output style (suppressed when value is "default" to reduce noise)
if [ -n "$skill_name" ] && [ "$skill_name" != "default" ]; then
  printf "  ${purple}skill:%s${reset}" "$skill_name"
fi

# objective + progress (only shown when session has been named via /rename)
if [ -n "$session_name" ]; then
  if [ -n "$progress_part" ]; then
    printf "  ${yellow}%s${reset}  ${gold}%s${reset}" "$session_name" "$progress_part"
  else
    printf "  ${yellow}%s${reset}" "$session_name"
  fi
fi

# context remaining
if [ -n "$ctx_part" ]; then
  printf "  ${ctx_color}%s${reset}" "$ctx_part"
fi

printf "\n"

content.content: The file /Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
The script has been updated. Here is a summary of exactly what changed:

**Removed**
- `hostname -s` call and the `user@host` format. The status line now shows just the username (`saurabhtyagi`) in grey.

**Added — Objective (session name)**
- Reads `.session_name` from the JSON input. This is populated whenever you run `/rename` inside a Claude session to name your current task/goal.
- Displayed in warm yellow (`ansi 221`) to stand out from the grey metadata.
- If the session has no name yet, nothing is printed for this slot — no noise.

**Added — Progress bar**
- Uses `.context_window.used_percentage` as a proxy for progress through the objective (how far into the context window the conversation has consumed).
- Renders a 10-block bar with filled (`█`) and empty (`░`) segments, followed by the numeric percentage, in gold (`ansi 179`).
- Only shown when a session name is present (progress without context is meaningless noise).

**Example of what the line looks like once a session is named**

```
saurabhtyagi  ~/code/myproject  main*  Claude Opus 4  refactor auth layer  ███░░░░░░░ 30%  ctx:70%
```

**How to set the objective**
Inside any Claude Code session, run `/rename refactor auth layer` (or whatever your current task is). The session name will immediately appear in the status line alongside the progress bar.

---

Note: for any further status line changes, use the `statusline-setup` sub-agent (the one that just ran this update). You can ask Claude in your main session to "update the status line to ..." and it will invoke this agent automatically. The script lives at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh` and the settings entry is in `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/settings.json`.
